content: I, Vikram Mehta, resident of House No. 12, Sector 48, Noida, Uttar Pradesh, hereby lodge this First Information Report regarding the kidnapping of my 12-year-old son, Arjun Mehta. On 15th October 2023, at approximately 15:05 hours, Arjun was taken from outside Noida Public School by two masked men on a motorcycle. The incident was witnessed by several school staff and students. One of the kidnappers forced Arjun into a black SUV parked near the school gate. The vehicle was registered to me, but I did not authorize its use at that time. Shortly after, I received a message via the encrypted app Signal demanding ₹2.5 crore in cash, to be delivered at the forested area near Sector 57 by 18:00 hours. The message threatened harm to Arjun if the police were involved. I immediately contacted the police and cooperated fully. Arjun’s smartwatch signal dropped at 15:10 near the Noida-Greater Noida Expressway. No ransom has been paid. I request immediate investigation and protection for my family. I have no knowledge of any person involved and have no grudges against anyone. I have attached copies of the ransom message and Arjun’s smartwatch location history.

I was cleaning near the main gate around 3:00 PM when I saw a man in a bright blue jacket pulling Arjun Mehta towards a black SUV. He had a cloth over his face, but his voice sounded familiar. Arjun was screaming, but the man slapped him and pushed him inside. The SUV drove off fast. I ran to the principal’s office but couldn’t stop them. The man wore blue — I’m sure of it. I didn’t see the other man. I didn’t know who it was, but I thought maybe someone from the staff? I told the police the same thing. I didn’t lie. I saw the blue jacket.

I was in Room 7B from 14:45 to 15:20. I wore a blue jacket that day — yes, it was the same one Ram Singh described. But I never left the classroom. My attendance register and CCTV from the corridor confirm this. I didn’t see Arjun being taken. I didn’t know anything about it. I only found out later when the school was locked down. I was confused when the police asked me about the blue jacket — I didn’t realize anyone could have mistaken me for the kidnapper. I never touched Arjun or his bag.

I was at my farmhouse in Ballabhgarh from 2:00 PM to 7:00 PM on October 15. My wife, Priya, and two guests, Rajiv and Ankit, can confirm. I didn’t know about the kidnapping until the news broke. I haven’t spoken to Vikram in over a year. I have no motive. I didn’t send any messages. I don’t even know how to use encrypted apps. My phone was stolen two days before — I reported it to the police on October 13. I don’t know why my phone’s signal was near Sector 57. I didn’t go there. I swear on my son’s life.

I was home all day on October 15. My wife cooked dinner at 7:00 PM. I watched TV from 5:00 PM onwards. I didn’t drive Vikram’s SUV that day. I didn’t know Arjun was taken. I didn’t call anyone. I didn’t go near the school. I have no idea who did it. I’ve been loyal to Vikram for 8 years. I’m just a driver. I don’t know anything about money or kidnapping. My phone was dead all day — I forgot to charge it. That’s why my wife says I was home — because I was.

I was at home with Rohan in Ballabhgarh on the 15th. I didn’t know anything about the kidnapping until I saw the news. I haven’t been to Vikram’s office since June. I didn’t print any documents. I didn’t know about the bank receipt. I didn’t speak to Manoj Verma in years. I was in a bad place emotionally after the divorce, but I would never hurt Arjun. He’s my son too. I love him. I don’t know why my DNA was on his bag. Maybe I hugged him that morning? I packed his lunch. Maybe I touched the strap. But I didn’t do anything illegal. I’m not a criminal.

I didn’t kidnap anyone. I’ve been in jail since October 12 for a petty theft charge — you can check the Noida Central Jail records. My motorcycle was stolen on October 13. I reported it to the police. I never saw it again. I don’t know who used it. I don’t know Ramesh Kumar. I don’t know Manoj Verma. I’m just a poor man trying to survive. I didn’t send any ransom messages. I don’t even have a phone right now. I didn’t go near Sector 62. I swear on my mother’s grave.

metadata.relation_to_case: Former Security Head, Noida Public School

content: I was fired from the school in August 2023 after I reported Vikram Mehta’s illegal land acquisition in Sector 45. I was framed for theft — I didn’t steal anything. I didn’t kidnap Arjun. I was at home on October 15. I didn’t go to the school. I didn’t know he was being taken. I didn’t know Ramesh Kumar was involved. I hate Vikram, yes, but I wouldn’t hurt a child. I’ve got a daughter of my own. I’m not a monster. I don’t know why the police think I’m involved. I didn’t use a burner phone. I don’t have one.

I had no idea anyone would target my son. I’ve been a law-abiding citizen. I don’t owe anyone money. I’ve never been involved in any illegal land deals — those are lies spread by Manoj Verma after he was fired. I haven’t spoken to Rohan in over a year — I deleted his number. I didn’t know about the calls until the police showed me the recovered logs. I didn’t know Priya had access to my printer. I didn’t know Ramesh had a tracker device. I didn’t know about the motorcycle. I’m devastated. I just want my son back. I’m cooperating fully. I’ve offered ₹5 crore reward for information. I have nothing to hide.

metadata.investigating_officer: SI Rajesh Kumar, Noida PS Sector 16

content: CCTV footage from School Gate Camera #4 shows two individuals approaching Arjun Mehta at 14:55:12. Both are dressed in black hoodies and masks. One individual (Tall, ~175 cm, slight limp) approaches Arjun from the left, speaking briefly. Arjun turns toward a black SUV (registration UP-12-AB-7891) parked 12 meters away. The second individual (Shorter, ~168 cm, broad shoulders) emerges from behind a bush and grabs Arjun’s arm. The victim is forced into the SUV by 14:57:03. The SUV departs at 14:57:45. The individual in the driver’s seat is later identified as Ramesh Kumar via facial recognition. No person in blue jacket appears in this footage. The janitor’s statement regarding a blue jacket is inconsistent with video evidence. The footage was not tampered with. Timestamps verified against NTP server.

metadata.investigating_officer: SI Rajesh Kumar, Noida PS Sector 16

content: CCTV footage from the Ghaziabad Mobile Shop shows a male purchasing a prepaid burner phone (IMEI: 867845032145678) at 16:28. The individual is wearing a cap, surgical mask, and dark jacket. Height estimated at 168-170 cm. Gait analysis indicates a slight right leg drag. Facial features obscured. The individual pays in cash (₹2,000). The shopkeeper identifies the gait as matching Ramesh Kumar’s walk, based on prior employment records from Vikram Mehta’s security detail. No resemblance to Bittu Singh. The purchaser exits on a red motorcycle (registration UP-12-CD-4567), later confirmed as belonging to Bittu Singh’s cousin, Ravi Singh.

metadata.investigating_officer: SI Rajesh Kumar, Noida PS Sector 16

content: CCTV footage from the pawnshop ‘Golden Key’ shows a male entering at 11:16 and returning a red motorcycle (UP-12-CD-4567) at 11:18. The individual is wearing a black hoodie and gloves. He hands over the keys and a ₹5,000 reward receipt. The pawnshop owner, Ramesh Patel, identifies the build and gait as matching Ramesh Kumar. The individual states, 'Found it near the expressway, thought I’d claim the reward.' The motorcycle was reported stolen on October 13 by Bittu Singh. The pawnshop owner was not aware of the kidnapping at the time. This footage was not disclosed in Bittu Singh’s original statement.

metadata.lab: Central Forensic Science Laboratory, New Delhi

content: The ransom note was printed on 80 gsm bond paper. Ink analysis reveals trace composition of 'Cyan 541' and 'Magenta 612', consistent with HP LaserJet Pro MFP M428fdw printer ink, not government-issued printers. The paper stock matches the exact batch used in Vikram Mehta’s private office (Batch #N-2023-09-11). No trace of Uttar Pradesh Revenue Department ink was found. The printer used was not a government model. The note was printed using a high-end office printer, likely accessed via a shared network. DNA on the paper’s edge (minor skin cells) matches Priya Mehta. The note was printed on October 15, 17:02:44, according to printer logs recovered from Vikram’s office server.

Forensic analysis of Arjun’s smartwatch reveals the GPS module was manually disabled via Bluetooth command at 15:05:17 — 5 minutes before the signal drop reported in the FIR. The command originated from a paired Bluetooth device with MAC address 1A:2B:3C:4D:5E:6F. This device matches the tracker unit installed in Vikram Mehta’s SUV (UP-12-AB-7891), which was removed by Ramesh Kumar during his tenure as a vehicle tracker technician. The device was later found in Ramesh Kumar’s residence. The smartwatch’s accelerometer shows no violent movement between 15:05 and 15:10, suggesting Arjun was not forcibly moved during this interval — he was likely sedated or drugged. The device was not remotely disabled by the kidnappers, but by someone with internal access to the vehicle’s tracking system.

metadata.institution: State Bank of India, Noida Branch

content: Bank statement for Account No. SBIN/9876543210 (Rohan Mehta) shows NO withdrawal of ₹20,00,000 on October 14, 2023. The so-called 'withdrawal receipt' submitted to police is a forgery. Paper stock matches Vikram Mehta’s office printer paper (Batch #N-2023-09-11). Ink composition matches the printer used in Vikram’s office. The bank letterhead used is a high-resolution scan of SBI’s official letterhead, obtained via phishing from a former SBI employee. Digital signature embedded is fake. The receipt was printed on October 15, 17:01:33, from Vikram Mehta’s office printer, logged under Priya Mehta’s user credentials. No transaction occurred. The receipt was created to frame Rohan Mehta.

Forensic recovery of deleted call logs reveals two calls from Vikram Mehta’s phone to an unlisted number (98765 43210) registered to Rohan Mehta on October 12, 2023, at 10:12 and 10:47. Duration: 27 seconds and 22 seconds. The calls were manually deleted from the device using a third-party app ('Call Eraser Pro'). The number was not in Vikram’s contact list. Call metadata shows the receiver was active in Ballabhgarh. The content of the calls was not recovered. This contradicts Vikram’s claim of 'no contact for over a year.' The calls occurred 3 days before the kidnapping. No further contact recorded.

This report summarizes the findings of the investigation into the kidnapping of Arjun Mehta. Key contradictions have been identified: 1) The blue jacket witness account is false; CCTV shows black attire. 2) The ₹20 lakh withdrawal receipt is forged, printed on Vikram’s printer by Priya Mehta. 3) The burner phone was purchased by Ramesh Kumar, not Bittu Singh. 4) The motorcycle was returned to the pawnshop by Ramesh Kumar on October 16. 5) GPS on Arjun’s watch was disabled by Ramesh Kumar using his old tracker device. 6) The ransom note ink does not match government printers — it matches Vikram’s office. 7) Two deleted calls from Vikram to Rohan indicate prior contact. 8) Priya Mehta’s DNA on Arjun’s bag suggests direct contact on the morning of the kidnapping. 9) Manoj Verma, recently terminated for exposing Vikram’s illegal land deals, has no alibi for 14:30-15:30 on October 15. 10) Ramesh Kumar’s alibi (TV logs) is fabricated — the TV was turned on remotely at 17:05. Based on the evidence, the prime suspects are Manoj Verma (primary perpetrator), Ramesh Kumar (accomplice), and Priya Mehta (forensic facilitator). The motive is revenge for Manoj’s termination and financial gain. Bittu Singh and Rohan Mehta are innocent, framed. The case has been escalated to the Special Task Force for further tracking of the escape route to Ballabhgarh.

IN THE COURT OF THE SESSIONS JUDGE, NOIDA

Charge Sheet No.: CS/2023/011

Accused: Manoj Verma, Ramesh Kumar, Priya Mehta

Sections: 364 (Kidnapping), 366 (Kidnapping to compel marriage or forced labor), 384 (Extortion), 465 (Forgery), 468 (Forgery for purpose of cheating), 471 (Using forged document), 201 (Destruction of evidence)

The accused, Manoj Verma, former security head of Noida Public School, conspired with Ramesh Kumar, the personal driver of Vikram Mehta, to kidnap Arjun Mehta to exact revenge for his wrongful termination after exposing Vikram Mehta’s illegal land acquisition in Sector 45. Priya Mehta, Vikram’s estranged wife and Manoj’s secret lover, forged the ₹20 lakh bank receipt to frame Rohan Mehta and diverted police attention. Ramesh Kumar used his knowledge of Arjun’s routine and vehicle tracking device to disable the smartwatch and lure Arjun into the SUV. The ransom note, printed using Vikram’s office printer, was designed to mislead investigators into believing government involvement. The stolen motorcycle was used as a decoy to implicate Bittu Singh. The accused fled via a pre-planned tunnel to a farmhouse in Ballabhgarh. Evidence includes CCTV, forensic ink analysis, digital forensics, and recovered tracker device. All accused are charged under IPC Sections as above. The prosecution seeks maximum punishment.
